info.name: Engine Validation Gating on Promotion — Validation
info.description:
Validates that the Content Manager only forwards a promotion payload to the local Wazuh Engine when the resulting destination space contains engine-related resources (decoders, kvdbs, or filters).

Background:
  The engine validation gate checks whether the RESULTING destination space after applying the promotion will contain any engine resources — not just whether the changeset itself adds them. This means:
    - A promotion that adds only integrations/rules still triggers engine validation if the destination already contains decoders, kvdbs, or filters from prior promotions.
    - A promotion that adds decoders/kvdbs/filters to an otherwise-empty destination also triggers engine validation.
    - Only when the resulting destination has none of these resource types is the engine call skipped.

Expected behavior under test:
  - Folder 1: DRAFT→TEST promotions whose CHANGESET has no engine resources AND the destination is empty → engine skipped, succeed without engine.
  - Folder 2: DRAFT→TEST promotions whose changeset ADDS engine resources → engine invoked.
  - Folder 3: DRAFT→TEST promotions whose changeset has NO engine resources, but the destination ALREADY has them → engine still invoked.

How to use:
  1. Import this collection.
  2. Set `baseUrl`, `cm_username`, `cm_password` in the collection variables (defaults: https://localhost:9200, admin/admin).
  3. Run folders 0 → 3 in order. Resource IDs are auto-captured.

Validating the gating with the engine OFF (recommended):
  After running folder 0 (bootstrap), stop the wazuh-engine service and re-run.
    - Folder 1 requests → 200 (engine skipped — correct).
    - Folder 2 requests → 500 (engine attempted, unreachable — correct).
    - Folder 3.1 → 500 (engine attempted, unreachable — correct; 200 means the engine was incorrectly skipped).
    - Folder 3.3 → 500 (engine attempted, unreachable — correct; if engine UP it may return 200 or 400).
  With the engine UP, folder 2 and 3 may return 200 (accepted) or 400 (engine rejected); any of these confirms the engine was reached.

item.name: 0. Bootstrap (DRAFT)
0.7 Create Integration C
item.description: Parent integration for Rule B — used in folder 3 to produce a changeset that has no engine resources while the destination already has them.

item.name: 0.8 Create Rule B (under Integration C)
item.description: Rule used in folder 3 promotions. It has no associated decoders/kvdbs/filters, so its promotion changeset is engine-resource-free.

item.name: 0.9 Set draft policy root_decoder to Decoder A
item.description:
Required so folder 2 engine promotions succeed. The engine validates the policy when decoders/kvdbs/filters are promoted: without a valid root_decoder it rejects every promotion (400), and the resources never land in TEST. Setting root_decoder to Decoder A makes folder 2 pass, populating TEST with the engine resources that folder 3 then relies on.

A pre-request script mirrors the current draft policy integrations and filters into the body so the validateListEquality check in the handler does not reject the update.

exec: pm.test('DRAFT policy root_decoder set to Decoder A', function () {

body.raw:
{
  "type": "policy",
  "resource": {
    "title": "Engine Gating Test Policy",
    "date": "2026-02-03T18:57:33.931731040Z",
    "modified": "2026-02-03T18:57:33.931731040Z",
    "enabled": false,
    "index_unclassified_events": false,
    "index_discarded_events": false,
    "root_decoder": "{{decoder_a_id}}",
    "integrations": {{_draft_integrations}},
    "filters": {{_draft_filters}},
    "enrichments": [],
    "author": "Engine Gating Tests",
    "description": "Sets root_decoder so folder 2 engine promotions succeed.",
    "documentation": "",
    "references": []
  }
}

item.description: Creates one resource per type in DRAFT. Each test in folders 1 and 2 promotes a distinct subset so ADD operations don't collide. Integration C and Rule B are created here for folder 3.

item.name: 1. Engine SKIPPED (no decoders/kvdbs/filters in changeset)

request.description: Promotes a single integration with no decoder/kvdb/filter activity. Engine is skipped. With the engine OFF, this request should still return 200.
Brings Integration A to TEST so subsequent rule/decoder/kvdb promotions have their parent in target. Engine is still skipped — this changeset has no decoders/kvdbs/filters.
Rules aren't part of the engine payload, so this promotion should succeed without engine.promote() being called.
A policy-only UPDATE has no decoder/kvdb/filter activity, so the engine call is gated off. The handler resolves the source policy by space, not by id, so 'policy' is a placeholder.

item.description: Each promotion in this folder lacks decoders, kvdbs, and filters in the changeset. With the gating fix in place, engine.promote() is never invoked and these requests succeed even when the engine is down.

item.name: 2. Engine INVOKED (decoders, kvdbs, or filters in changeset)

exec: console.log('Engine appears unreachable or rejected — confirms gating still routes decoder changes through engine.promote().');

request.description: Decoders are part of the engine payload — gating condition forwards the changeset to engine.promote(). Any of {200, 400, 500} confirms the engine was reached.

exec: console.log('Engine appears unreachable or rejected — confirms gating still routes kvdb changes through engine.promote().');

request.description: KVDBs are part of the engine payload — gating still routes this through engine.promote(). Any of {200, 400, 500} confirms the engine was reached.

exec: console.log('Engine appears unreachable or rejected — confirms gating still routes filter changes through engine.promote().');

request.description: Filters are part of the engine payload — gating still routes this through engine.promote(). Any of {200, 400, 500} confirms the engine was reached.

item.description: Each promotion in this folder includes a decoder, kvdb, or filter. The gating condition leaves the engine call in place, so the outcome depends on engine reachability.

item.name: 3. Engine INVOKED via existing destination engine resources
3.1 Set invalid root_decoder in DRAFT policy
item.description:
Sets root_decoder to a syntactically valid but nonexistent decoder UUID. The Content Manager API accepts it (non-empty UUID format), but the engine will reject at validation time because no decoder with that ID exists.

A pre-request script fetches the current DRAFT policy integrations and filters, then rewrites the request body with those preserved values. This satisfies the validateListEquality check in the handler (no additions or removals), while still changing root_decoder.

If this step returns 400 it means the script failed to read the current policy state — check the collection variable base URL and credentials.

exec: // Fetch the current DRAFT policy so we can mirror its integrations and filters.
// updatePolicy() only allows reordering — not adding or removing — so we must
// echo back the same arrays while only changing root_decoder.
pm.test('DRAFT policy updated (invalid root_decoder set)', function () {

body.raw:
{
  "type": "policy",
  "resource": {
    "title": "Engine Gating Test Policy",
    "date": "2026-02-03T18:57:33.931731040Z",
    "modified": "2026-02-03T18:57:33.931731040Z",
    "enabled": false,
    "index_unclassified_events": false,
    "index_discarded_events": false,
    "root_decoder": "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000",
    "integrations": {{_draft_integrations}},
    "filters": {{_draft_filters}},
    "enrichments": [],
    "author": "Engine Gating Tests",
    "description": "DRAFT policy with a nonexistent root_decoder — engine must reject this.",
    "documentation": "",
    "references": []
  }
}

item.name: 3.2 Promote Integration C + Rule B — engine INVOKED (destination has Decoder A, invalid root_decoder → fails)
item.description:
Core scenario: the changeset has NO engine resources (only an integration + rule), but the destination space (TEST) already contains Decoder A (from 2.1), KVDB A (from 2.2), and Filter A (from 2.3). The resulting destination space will contain engine resources, so engine.promote() MUST be called.

The default draft/test policy has an empty root_decoder. When the engine validates a space that holds decoders but the policy has no root_decoder, it rejects (400). If the engine is unreachable it returns 500. Either outcome confirms the engine was reached.

A 200 response signals the BUG: the gating condition only checked the changeset (no decoders/kvdbs/filters in the diff) and skipped the engine call, without considering that the destination ALREADY holds engine resources.

Prerequisite: folders 0–2 must have run and 2.1/2.2/2.3 must have returned 200 (i.e., the engine accepted the promotions so the resources were actually consolidated into TEST).

exec: pm.test('Engine was NOT skipped — response must not be 200', function () {

'200 means the engine was bypassed even though the destination space already contains engine resources (decoders/kvdbs/filters). The gating condition must check the RESULTING destination state, not just the changeset.');
console.log('Engine UP and correctly rejected: empty root_decoder with decoders present in destination.');
console.log('Engine DOWN (500) — but the engine call was still correctly attempted (not skipped).');

item.name: 3.3 Restore root_decoder in DRAFT policy
item.description: Reverts root_decoder back to Decoder A so subsequent operations are not affected. At this point the draft policy has no integrations or filters (set to empty in 3.1), so a fixed body is sufficient.

body.raw:
{
  "type": "policy",
  "resource": {
    "title": "Engine Gating Test Policy",
    "date": "2026-02-03T18:57:33.931731040Z",
    "modified": "2026-02-03T18:57:33.931731040Z",
    "enabled": false,
    "index_unclassified_events": false,
    "index_discarded_events": false,
    "root_decoder": "{{decoder_a_id}}",
    "integrations": [],
    "filters": [],
    "enrichments": [],
    "author": "Engine Gating Tests",
    "description": "Restore root_decoder after folder 3 test.",
    "documentation": "",
    "references": []
  }
}

item.name: 3.4 Promote DELETE of Decoder A — engine still INVOKED (KVDB A and Filter A remain in destination)
item.description: Verifies that a DELETE operation in the changeset does not bypass engine validation when other engine resources remain in the resulting destination. After deleting Decoder A, TEST still contains KVDB A (from 2.2) and Filter A (from 2.3), so engine.promote() MUST still be invoked. Any of {200, 400, 500} confirms the engine was reached.

exec: pm.test('Engine was NOT skipped — remaining destination engine resources still trigger validation', function () {
console.log('Engine UP and accepted: deleting a decoder while KVDB+Filter remain still invokes engine.');
console.log('Engine down or rejected — but engine call was still correctly attempted (not skipped).');

item.description:
These promotions have NO engine resources in the changeset, but the destination space (TEST) already contains Decoder A, KVDB A, and Filter A from folder 2. Because the resulting destination space still contains engine resources, engine.promote() MUST be invoked.

Key invariant under test:
  The gating condition evaluates the RESULTING destination space — not just the promotion changeset. An integration-only or rule-only promotion is NOT engine-free when the destination already holds decoders, kvdbs, or filters.

Prerequisite: folder 2 (2.1, 2.2, 2.3) must have returned 200 so that resources were actually consolidated into TEST. If those requests returned 400/500, the TEST space has no engine resources and this folder's tests are inconclusive.
